Items.Key: command injection via admin execute command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec' followed by any shell command string|validate and sanitize the command input before execution. use a whitelist of allowed commands or use a safe api instead of system().->command injection via admin execute command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec' followed by any shell command string|validate and sanitize the command input before execution. use a whitelist of allowed commands or use a safe api instead of system().

command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|624|633|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; #endif system(command.c_str());|an attacker can manipulate environment variables (temp/tmpdir) to redirect the audit log file, potentially leading to arbitrary command execution.|environment variable temp or tmpdir set to a path containing shell metacharacters like '; rm -rf /'|sanitize environment variables and audit paths. use a fixed, safe directory for audit logs. avoid using system() with constructed commands.->command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|624|633|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; #endif system(command.c_str());|an attacker can manipulate environment variables (temp/tmpdir) to redirect the audit log file, potentially leading to arbitrary command execution.|environment variable temp or tmpdir set to a path containing shell metacharacters like '; rm -rf /'|sanitize environment variables and audit paths. use a fixed, safe directory for audit logs. avoid using system() with constructed commands.

buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|692|694|calculator::supercalc::run|char formatted_result[64]; snprintf(formatted_result, sizeof(formatted_result), "%.10g", result); sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|the sprintf call can overflow the 512-byte result_buffer_ if formatted_result exceeds expected bounds.|any calculation result that produces a formatted string longer than 64 characters|use snprintf instead of sprintf: snprintf(result_buffer_, sizeof(result_buffer_), "result: %s", formatted_result);->buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|692|694|calculator::supercalc::run|char formatted_result[64]; snprintf(formatted_result, sizeof(formatted_result), "%.10g", result); sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|the sprintf call can overflow the 512-byte result_buffer_ if formatted_result exceeds expected bounds.|any calculation result that produces a formatted string longer than 64 characters|use snprintf instead of sprintf: snprintf(result_buffer_, sizeof(result_buffer_), "result: %s", formatted_result);
